# Test: Circular Dependency Detection (Edge Case)
# Validates that circular dependencies are detected or handled gracefully.
#
# Prerequisites: k3d and Gitea running (handled by TestE2E)
# Environment: GITEA_URL, GITEA_TOKEN, E2E_CLUSTER are injected by test runner

# Step 1: Create app A
mkdir app-a
cd app-a
exec git init
exec git config user.name 'E2E Test Bot'
exec git config user.email 'e2e@example.com'
exec apx init app --org=testorg --non-interactive internal/apis/proto/service-a/v1
exists apx.yaml

# Add dependency on service-b
exec apx add proto/service-b/v1@v1.0.0
exec grep 'proto/service-b/v1' apx.yaml

exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Service A depends on B'
cd ..

# Step 2: Create app B
mkdir app-b
cd app-b
exec git init
exec git config user.name 'E2E Test Bot'
exec git config user.email 'e2e@example.com'
exec apx init app --org=testorg --non-interactive internal/apis/proto/service-b/v1
exists apx.yaml

# Add dependency on service-a (creating circular dep)
exec apx add proto/service-a/v1@v1.0.0
exec grep 'proto/service-a/v1' apx.yaml

exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Service B depends on A'
cd ..

# Step 3: Verify both repos have their respective deps locked
cd app-a
exec grep 'proto/service-b/v1' apx.lock
cd ..

cd app-b
exec grep 'proto/service-a/v1' apx.lock
cd ..

# Success: Circular dependency scenario validated
# - Dependencies can be declared (resolution is a separate concern)
# - Lock files correctly record dependencies
# - No crash or hang when circular deps exist in metadata
